sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: UFP Packaging acquires Berry Pallets to expand Midwest presence By Investing.com
feed.summary: UFP Packaging, a division of UFP Industries, has acquired Berry Pallets, Inc., a pallet manufacturer in Waseca, Minnesota, to expand its presence in the Upper Midwest. This acquisition aligns with UFP Industries' strategy of investing in higher-margin businesses and expanding manufacturing capacity, supported by a strong balance sheet. The financial terms were not disclosed, but other recent news suggests the acquisition was for approximately $20 million, adding $23 million in annual sales and 75 employees.

feed.title: Nomad Foods Price Targets Decline Amid Rising Input Costs
feed.summary: Barclays and Deutsche Bank have lowered their price targets for Nomad Foods Limited (NYSE:NOMD) due to concerns about rising input costs and geopolitical instability. Barclays reduced its target from $13 to $12 while maintaining an Overweight rating, citing higher input costs impacting the food sector. Deutsche Bank downgraded the company from Buy to Hold, lowering its target from $15 to $10, highlighting cost pressure from the Iran conflict and potential demand headwinds in Europe.

feed.title: Apple’s leadership transition, AI vision under new CEO Ternus to steal spotlight during earnings (AAPL:NASDAQ)
feed.summary: Apple's upcoming quarterly results and earnings call are anticipated to focus on the tech giant's leadership transition, with Tim Cook stepping down as CEO in September, and the company's artificial intelligence strategy under incoming CEO John Ternus. Analysts expect robust performance driven by strong demand for iPhones, Macs, and Services, especially in China, and new product launches. The new CEO is expected to emphasize hardware innovation and deeper AI integration to maintain growth and competitive advantage.

feed.title: Air Products Industrial Gas Supply for Samsung Semiconductor Fab in South Korea
feed.summary: Air Products (NYSE:APD) has been selected by Samsung to supply industrial gases for its new advanced semiconductor fab in Pyeongtaek, Gyeonggi Province, South Korea. This initiative involves Air Products building, owning, and operating multiple production facilities and a bulk specialty gas supply system. This project is Air Products' largest investment in the semiconductor industry to date and will establish Pyeongtaek as its biggest operations site globally for electronics.

feed.title: American Water Works (AWK) Announces Dividend Increase to $0.895 Per Share
feed.summary: American Water Works Co Inc (AWK) has declared an 8.2% dividend increase to $0.895 per share, signaling confidence in its financial health. The company, the largest investor-owned water and wastewater utility in the U.S., holds a strong GF Score™ of 89/100, driven by high profitability and growth, though its financial strength is rated lower. Investors should consider these mixed signals, alongside minimal insider buying, when evaluating the stock.

feed.title: Samsung taps Air Products for gases at new South Korea chip fab
feed.summary: Air Products (NYSE:APD) has been selected by Samsung to supply industrial gases for its new advanced semiconductor fab in Pyeongtaek, South Korea. This agreement marks Air Products' largest investment to date in the semiconductor industry, establishing Pyeongtaek as its single largest global operations site for electronics. The new facilities will supply nitrogen, oxygen, argon, and hydrogen, coming online in phases between 2028 and 2030.

feed.title: A Minnesota pallet maker gives UFP Packaging an Upper Midwest base
feed.summary: UFP Packaging, a division of UFP Industries (NASDAQ: UFPI), has acquired Berry Pallets, Inc., a pallet manufacturer in Waseca, Minnesota. This acquisition expands UFP Packaging's presence into the Upper Midwest, aligning with its strategy to invest in core, higher-margin businesses and enhance manufacturing capacity for national customers. The deal, valued at $20 million with Berry Pallets reporting $23 million in annual sales, is expected to preserve existing operations while providing broader resources and stability.

feed.title: Schwab Small Cap ETF Tops State Street in Returns and Diversification
feed.summary: This article compares two small-cap ETFs, Schwab U.S. Small-Cap ETF (SCHA) and State Street SPDR Portfolio S&P 600 Small Cap ETF (SPSM), highlighting their differences in diversification, expense ratios, returns, and fundamental strategies. SCHA offers broader market exposure with over 1,700 holdings and higher 1-year returns, while SPSM has a slightly lower expense ratio, a higher dividend yield, and screens for profitable companies. The choice between them depends on an investor's philosophical preference for maximum market breadth versus a quality-controlled portfolio.

feed.title: Waste Management ticks lower as Q1 profit rose but collection volumes fell (WM:NYSE)
feed.summary: Waste Management (WM) shares ticked lower despite reporting better-than-expected Q1 adjusted earnings and reaffirming its FY 2026 revenue guidance. The company's Q1 profit increased to $723 million, or $1.79/share, from $637 million, or $1.58/share, in the previous year, but collection volumes declined.

feed.title: GE HealthCare stock falls on guidance cut (GEHC:NASDAQ)
feed.summary: GE HealthCare (GEHC) stock dropped approximately 13% to a one-year low after the company announced mixed Q1 2026 results and reduced its full-year earnings outlook. The guidance cut was primarily attributed to higher-than-anticipated inflationary pressures.

feed.title: CT’s top court sides with regulators in Eversource emergency response case
feed.summary: Connecticut's Supreme Court unanimously ruled that Eversource Energy cannot challenge a state regulator's order to tighten emergency response standards. The ruling upholds a 30-minute response target imposed after a January 2023 accident where first responders waited over an hour for Eversource. The court rejected Eversource's argument that it was denied a fair hearing, stating that the right to appeal is limited to "contested cases" which did not apply in this instance.

feed.title: Valero Energy rises as refining margins stay elevated and traders position ahead of earnings
feed.summary: Valero Energy (VLO) stock rose 3.8% due to strengthened refining margins, possibly linked to Middle East supply disruptions and higher energy prices. Traders are also positioning ahead of Valero's upcoming quarterly results, with some confidence that operational disruptions, such as a recent refinery incident, are becoming manageable. The company is set to report earnings before market open on April 30, 2026.

feed.title: ONEOK sees higher earnings
feed.summary: ONEOK, Inc. reported a significant increase in first-quarter earnings for 2026, with net income surging by 12% to $776 million and adjusted EBITDA up by 13% to $2.0 billion. In response to this strong performance and a constructive market environment, the Tulsa-based company has raised its 2026 financial guidance, projecting higher net income and adjusted EBITDA. Despite the increased financial outlook, the total 2026 capital expenditure guidance remains unchanged.

feed.title: Unum (UNM) Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript
feed.summary: Unum Group reported strong first-quarter 2026 results with after-tax adjusted operating EPS up nearly 10% year-over-year, driven by robust sales growth in its U.S. and international markets. The company maintained strong capital metrics, exceeding its target RBC ratio, and aggressively repurchased shares. Management also highlighted ongoing efforts to reduce risk in the Closed Block, including the termination of 7% of group long-term care cases.

feed.title: Earnings call transcript: Old Dominion Freight Line beats Q1 2026 forecasts
feed.summary: Old Dominion Freight Line (ODFL) reported better-than-expected Q1 2026 earnings, with EPS of $1.14 beating forecasts of $1.05 and revenue of $1.33 billion exceeding estimates of $1.31 billion. Despite a 2.9% year-over-year revenue decline, the company's stock rose 1.9% pre-market due to strong pricing execution and strategic investments. Executives noted improved sequential volume trends and a focus on maintaining service quality and market share gains, forecasting continued optimism for Q2 2026 with projected EPS of $1.32 and revenue of $1.44 billion.

feed.title: AMAZON COM INC : Receives a Buy rating from UBS
feed.summary: UBS has issued a Buy rating for Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) and maintained its target price at $304. The e-commerce and cloud computing giant continues to be viewed positively by the analyst firm. This announcement was made on April 29, 2026.

feed.title: Why Vertex Pharmaceuticals Stock Looks Undervalued in 2026
feed.summary: Vertex Pharmaceuticals (VRTX) reported strong 2025 results, with 9% revenue growth driven by its cystic fibrosis franchise and new commercial products like CASGEVY and JOURNAVX. The company's pipeline, particularly the IgA nephropathy drug povetacicept (pove), is generating significant analyst interest and upside potential. Despite these positive indicators and a TIKR model target of $835, the stock appears undervalued, trading at a forward EPS of approximately 23x, suggesting substantial future growth opportunities are being overlooked by the market.

feed.title: Grainger investors back auditor, pay and 12 directors at meeting
feed.summary: W.W. Grainger, Inc. (NYSE: GWW) shareholders elected 12 directors to its board and approved the ratification of Ernst & Young LLP as the independent auditor for fiscal year 2026. Additionally, shareholders voted in favor of an advisory say-on-pay resolution concerning executive compensation. Chairman and CEO D.G. Macpherson also provided an update on the company's 2025 financial and operational highlights during the virtual annual meeting.

feed.title: Franklin Resources (RNW) holds 14.6M Class A shares, 5.9% ownership
feed.summary: Franklin Resources Inc. and its affiliated investment managers have reported a beneficial ownership of 14,611,092 Class A Ordinary Shares of ReNew Energy Global plc (RNW), constituting 5.9% of the class as of March 31, 2026. This disclosure was made through a Schedule 13G SEC filing, indicating a passive investment. The filing also clarifies an internal realignment where previous separate reports from certain affiliates are now aggregated under Franklin Resources.

feed.title: Fifth Third Bank opens private bank office in Birmingham
feed.summary: Fifth Third Bank has opened a new Private Bank office in Birmingham, Alabama, expanding its strategic presence in the region by offering comprehensive wealth management solutions for high-net-worth individuals and families. This new office builds on the bank's existing commercial banking operations in Birmingham, which launched in 2024, and is staffed by experienced financial professionals including Cory L. Wilson, Marc Dobson, Greg Rudd, and Melissa Humber Yan. The expansion underscores Fifth Third's commitment to delivering personalized financial strategies and meeting the evolving needs of clients across Alabama.

feed.title: Sensata Technologies (NYSE:ST) Price Target Raised to $42.00 at Wells Fargo & Company
feed.summary: Wells Fargo & Company has raised its price target for Sensata Technologies (NYSE:ST) from $37.00 to $42.00, maintaining an "equal weight" rating. This new target suggests a potential upside of 4.61% from the stock's current price. Other analysts have also adjusted price targets, with an average "Hold" rating and a target price of $41.90 for the company.

feed.title: Grupo Supervielle (SUPV) Projected to Post Quarterly Earnings on Wednesday
feed.summary: Grupo Supervielle (SUPV) is anticipated to report its Q1 2026 earnings on Wednesday, May 6th, with analysts forecasting an EPS of $0.03 and revenues of $198.04 million. The company's stock is currently trading around $8.61, reflecting a mixed market view and several recent analyst downgrades. SUPV has a negative P/E ratio and an average analyst rating of "Hold" with a price target of $13.00.

feed.title: Polen Capital Adds Rollins Inc Position Amid Market Volatility
feed.summary: Polen Capital initiated a new position in Rollins, Inc. (NYSE:ROL) during the first quarter of 2026, despite its Focus Growth Strategy experiencing a decline in a volatile market. The investment firm highlighted Rollins' consistent growth, high customer retention, and significant expansion opportunities in the fragmented pest control industry. Rollins, Inc. maintains a market capitalization of $26.84 billion and analysts see potential for future growth under its new CEO.

feed.title: Franklin Resources (NYSE: PHM) reports 7.8% stake in PulteGroup
feed.summary: Franklin Resources, Inc. has reported a beneficial ownership of 7.8% in PulteGroup (PHM), totaling 14,978,904 common shares as of March 31, 2026. This stake is held across Franklin's investment management subsidiaries for their clients, with Putnam Investment Management, LLC holding the largest portion. The filing, a Schedule 13G/A, indicates an internal realignment that led to the aggregation of previously disaggregated holdings from affiliates like Franklin Mutual Advisers and Brandywine Global.

feed.title: Yum Brands wants AI and beverages to supercharge growth
feed.summary: Yum Brands plans to leverage artificial intelligence through its Byte system and introduce new menu innovations, particularly in beverages, to attract more customers and boost growth. The company is implementing AI-powered dynamic menu boards at Taco Bell and using a "global innovation pantry" at KFC to share successful limited-time offers and menu items across markets. While Taco Bell and The Habit Burger Grill saw sales growth, KFC and Pizza Hut experienced declines, indicating a need for these new strategies.

feed.title: Fund Update: 327,387 INTUIT (INTU) shares added to FLOSSBACH VON STORCH SE portfolio
feed.summary: FLOSSBACH VON STORCH SE has increased its holdings in INTUIT (INTU) by adding 327,387 shares to its portfolio, as reported in a recent SEC 13F filing for the Q1 2026 period. The article also details significant institutional investor activity, insider trading, congressional stock trading, and current analyst ratings and price targets for INTU stock.

feed.title: Vulcan Materials beats revenue estimates on strong demand for construction materials By Reuters
feed.summary: Vulcan Materials surpassed Wall Street's first-quarter revenue estimates due to robust demand for its construction materials, including crushed stone, sand, and gravel. The company's shares rose 4% as increased spending on public infrastructure, data centers, and energy projects boosted sales. Total revenue climbed 7.4% to $1.76 billion, exceeding expectations, and adjusted profit per share also saw a significant increase.

feed.title: UBS reiterates Buy on Sysco stock on volume growth improvement
feed.summary: UBS has reiterated a Buy rating and a $90.00 price target on Sysco (NYSE:SYY) due to continued operational improvements in the third quarter. Sysco reported a strong US local case volume growth of 3.3% and anticipates reaching its full-period goal of 2.5%+ local case growth for the fourth quarter. Despite missing some Q3 fiscal 2026 earnings estimates, analysts view the stock's current valuation as a favorable risk/reward profile.

feed.title: Uber adds hotel bookings as part of its goal to be the app for everything (UBER:NYSE)
feed.summary: Uber Technologies (UBER) has announced a new feature allowing users to book hotels through its app, expanding its services beyond ride-hailing and food delivery. This move is part of the company's broader strategy to become a comprehensive "app for everything." The hotel booking feature is facilitated through a partnership with Expedia Group (EXPE).

feed.title: UAMY Stock Pulls Back As Traders Weigh Rich Valuation
feed.summary: United States Antimony Corporation (NYSE: UAMY) stock has pulled back by 9.39% due to concerns over its antimony production outlook and a rich valuation. Despite strong revenue growth and a clean balance sheet for a micro-cap, the company is not yet profitable, leading to negative margins and returns. Traders are closely watching its volatile price action, which recently saw a significant surge followed by a cool-off, as they weigh future expectations against current earnings power.

feed.title: Smokey Bones closes Cincinnati-area restaurant amid nationwide closures
feed.summary: Smokey Bones, a barbecue chain, has closed its lone Greater Cincinnati restaurant in West Chester, along with several other locations nationwide. The closures follow Chapter 11 bankruptcy filing by its owner, Twin Peaks Hospitality, and plans to rebrand some Smokey Bones as Twin Peaks restaurants. Only four other Smokey Bones locations remain in Ohio.

feed.title: D. Boral Capital Acted as Exclusive Placement Agent to Trio-Tech International (NYSE: TRT) in Connection with its ~$10,000,000 Registered Direct Offering
feed.summary: D. Boral Capital LLC acted as the exclusive placement agent for Trio-Tech International's (NYSE: TRT) Registered Direct Offering, which closed on April 27, 2026. The offering involved the sale of 1,052,632 shares of common stock, generating approximately $10,000,000 in gross proceeds for Trio-Tech. Trio-Tech International is a global provider of semiconductor back-end solutions, while D. Boral Capital LLC is a New York-based investment bank specializing in strategic advisory and financial solutions.

feed.title: Is PulteGroup, Inc. (PHM) Among the Best Housing Stocks to Buy in 2026?
feed.summary: PulteGroup, Inc. (PHM) is being evaluated as a potential housing stock investment for 2026 despite weaker first-quarter results that saw declines in profit, revenue, and gross margin due to falling demand. The company is part of Insider Monkey's list of 10 Best Housing Stocks to Buy in 2026, and UBS recently raised its price target to $162 while maintaining a Buy rating. Despite these considerations, Insider Monkey suggests other AI stocks might offer greater return potential in a shorter timeframe.

feed.title: Costco adding new option to $1.50 hot dog combo
feed.summary: Costco is now offering water as an alternative to soda with its popular $1.50 hot dog combo, providing more versatility for customers. This change has already been implemented in some warehouses, including Hawaii, and seen on Reddit in other U.S. locations. The price of the beloved hot dog combo is guaranteed to remain $1.50 by CEO Ron Vachris.

feed.title: Trio-Tech sells 1.05M shares to raise about $10M in offering
feed.summary: Trio-Tech International (NYSE: TRT) announced the closing of a Registered Direct Offering on April 27, 2026, selling 1,052,632 shares of common stock to raise approximately $10 million in gross proceeds before commissions and expenses. D. Boral Capital acted as the exclusive placement agent for the offering, which was conducted under an existing Form S-3 shelf registration. The offering creates some dilution for existing shareholders, but the funds are expected to support the company's AI and automotive growth initiatives.

feed.title: Phillips 66 Beats Q1 Estimates by $0.88 Per Share as Refining Margins Surge
feed.summary: Phillips 66 (NYSE:PSX) exceeded Q1 earnings estimates by $0.88 per share, reporting adjusted earnings of $0.49 per share against a consensus loss of $0.39. The strong performance, driven by soaring refining margins amid a Middle East conflict, resulted in a net income of $207 million. The company also highlighted expansions in its Sweeny NGL fractionation and Freeport LPG export capacities, alongside ongoing projects like the Coastal Bend NGL pipeline expansion and a proposed NGL fractionator in Corpus Christi.

feed.title: As DTE seeks $474 million rate hike, Nessel pledges to intervene
feed.summary: Michigan Attorney General Dana Nessel is vowing to fight DTE Energy's latest request for a $474 million electrical rate increase for residential customers, which would be a 9.7% hike. Nessel, a frequent critic of the company, argues that DTE consistently seeks rate increases and treats Michigan families like an "open checkbook" to satisfy shareholders. She also expressed concerns about heavily redacted contracts related to a controversial data center project in Saline Township, which DTE claims will be key to a two-year rate increase pause if it comes online as planned.

feed.title: Marshalls store coming to Malone
feed.summary: Marshalls is set to open a new retail store in Malone, occupying the former Big Lots location. This move marks another expansion for TJX Companies, which operates several well-known retail chains. While an official opening date is yet to be confirmed, the company is actively seeking a store manager for the new location.

feed.title: Public Storage (PSA) FFO Near US$773m Tests Bullish Margin Resilience Narrative
feed.summary: Public Storage (PSA) reported Q1 2026 revenue of US$1.2 billion and FFO of US$772.7 million. While FFO remained stable, the net profit margin decreased from 37.4% to 35% year-over-year, challenging the bullish narrative of margin resilience. The article delves into bull and bear arguments, contrasting the company's discounted valuation and growth forecasts with concerns about declining earnings and high debt levels.

feed.title: DexCom (DXCM) Q1 Earnings: What To Expect
feed.summary: DexCom (DXCM) is set to report its Q1 earnings this Thursday, with analysts anticipating a 13.5% year-on-year revenue growth. The medical device company has a history of beating revenue expectations, and positive sentiment exists within the healthcare equipment and supplies segment despite DexCom's recent share price dip. Investors are keen to see if DexCom can maintain its consistent performance this quarter.

feed.title: Is Roper Technologies (ROP) Still Attractive After Recent Share Price Weakness?
feed.summary: Roper Technologies (ROP) stock has seen significant declines over the past year, prompting an analysis of its current valuation. According to a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model and Price/Earnings (P/E) ratio comparison, the stock appears to be undervalued by approximately 42.8% and also trades below its "Fair Ratio." Despite recent weakness tied to sector re-evaluation, the company's underlying value propositions suggest it might still be an attractive investment.

feed.title: A Look At CenterPoint Energy (CNP) Valuation After Recent Share Price Momentum
feed.summary: CenterPoint Energy (CNP) has shown recent stock price momentum, closing at $43.13, with an 11.36% year-to-date total return. Despite being deemed 6.5% undervalued with a fair value of $46.13 and future EPS growth projections, its P/E ratio of 26.3x is higher than industry averages, suggesting potential valuation risk. Investors are encouraged to consider both the growth narrative and valuation risks, exploring the full picture before making investment decisions.

feed.title: Evercore ISI Maintains an “In Line” Rating on Lowe’s Companies, Inc. (LOW)
feed.summary: Evercore ISI has maintained an "In Line" rating for Lowe's Companies, Inc. (LOW), raising its price target to $250 from $245. This decision comes as Lowe's expands its partnership with Relex Solutions to integrate AI for inventory planning and restocking, aiming for improved efficiency and real-time decision-making, with a full rollout expected by early 2027.

feed.title: Vanguard reports 7.7% stake in Newmont (NEM) — 83.2M shares disclosed
feed.summary: Vanguard Capital Management has reported a 7.7% stake in Newmont

feed.title: Vulcan Materials beats revenue estimates on strong demand for construction materials
feed.summary: Vulcan Materials (VMC.N) surpassed Wall Street's first-quarter revenue expectations, driven by robust demand for its construction materials like crushed stone, sand, and gravel. The building supplies maker's shares climbed 4% in morning trading as increased spending on public infrastructure, data centers, and energy projects fueled demand, despite rising input costs. The company reported a 7.4% rise in total revenue to $1.76 billion, exceeding anticipated figures, and its adjusted profit per share reached $1.26.

feed.title: What's in Store For These 4 Construction Stocks This Earnings Season?
feed.summary: The U.S. construction sector faces a mixed earnings season, with strong public infrastructure spending countering a weak housing market due to high mortgage rates and low consumer sentiment. Companies like MasTec and Quanta Services are poised to benefit from public projects and increased power demand, while Weyerhaeuser and Martin Marietta Materials are challenged by the housing downturn. Investors are watching for insights into demand, pricing, and backlog visibility from key players reporting earnings.

feed.title: Generac surges after raising full-year sales forecast on growing data center backlog (GNRC:NYSE)
feed.summary: Generac Holdings Inc. (GNRC) stock surged 14.5% to its highest intraday level in over three and a half years after beating Q1 adjusted earnings expectations. The company raised its full-year sales forecast, driven by strong demand for backup generators and a growing backlog, particularly from data centers. This positive outlook signals confidence in continued performance.

feed.title: Cooper Companies stock hits 52-week low at 61.76 USD
feed.summary: Cooper Companies (COO) recently hit a 52-week low of $61.76, marking a 23% year-to-date decline, although InvestingPro data suggests the stock is undervalued and in oversold territory, with management aggressively buying back shares. Despite this downturn, the company reported strong Q1 fiscal 2026 earnings, surpassing expectations and raising full-year guidance, leading analysts like Needham and Barclays to increase their price targets. These positive financial and strategic developments indicate potential for recovery despite current market pressures.

feed.title: Amex pushes for agentic standards
feed.summary: American Express is advocating for a shared industry standard in agentic commerce, demonstrating its commitment by offering consumer protections for bot-driven purchases. Despite rivals Visa and Mastercard also developing their own protocols, Amex emphasizes the need for collective interoperability to ensure trust, transparency, and accountability across the payments ecosystem. The company believes standardized practices are essential for the nascent agentic payments sector to grow and handle potential disputes effectively.

feed.title: Earnings call transcript: IDEX beats Q1 2026 forecasts, stock jumps
feed.summary: IDEX Corporation reported strong Q1 2026 results, surpassing EPS and revenue forecasts, which led to a 7.21% stock surge in pre-market trading. The company raised its full-year EPS guidance for 2026, driven by robust performance in its Health & Science Technologies segment and strategic focus on advantage markets. Executives highlighted consistent innovation, effective capital deployment, and the strong backlog in HST as key factors for continued optimism despite some geopolitical uncertainties.
